description.value:
A white-feathered cloak lined with names in invisible ink. It grants advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks in crowds.

Curse. Each long rest, one NPC who knew the wearer forgets their name and replaces it with a wrong one. Major allies resist once with a DC 20 Wisdom save. When the curse fully matures, written records omit the wearer unless witnessed in person that day.

unidentified.description: A pale feathered cloak. Crowds seem to part around you a little too cleanly.

cursedMeta.decoyAppearance: Legendary cloak of feathered anonymity in busy streets.
cursedMeta.trueNature: Each long rest an NPC forgets the wearer's true name; allies may resist once (DC 20 Wis). Records eventually omit the wearer without fresh witness.

curse.name: Unlisted Names
curse.description: After each long rest the GM removes one NPC's accurate memory of the wearer's name (replaced with a wrong name). Allied NPCs and PCs may attempt DC 20 Wisdom saving throw once per tier of the curse's progress to resist. At full activation written records about the wearer fade unless someone witnesses them that day.

hints: Your signature on old contracts looks like a stranger's hand.

escalation.effect: Barkeeps misfile your tab under a cousin you do not have.
Childhood friend uses a nickname you never gave them.
Official writs list your profession but leave the name blank.

removal.customRequirement: Publicly speak your true name before three witnesses who then burn a written oath, or end the curse with wish-level magic.
